taxonomy.why: Launch-readiness baselines stay replay-stable across flagship, mini, and coding routes.
Queued follow-up and chunk delivery remain a core runtime execution-chain contract.
Recovery samples are governed by failure fidelity, live probes, and promotion blockers.
Dedicated runtime binding and anti-reuse behavior remain mandatory for proving execution isolation.
Low-risk write paths stay gated by machine-readable workspace evidence.
Routing and placement edges stay covered as compatibility behavior evolves.
Runtime-only launch truth and continuity summaries are part of the core proving set.

modelProfiles.notes: Low-cost replay lane kept on the current official mini track.
Compatibility coding lane for runtime execution regressions.

capabilityCatalog.notes: Structured runtime truth stays independent from transcript equality.
Checkpoint, mission linkage, publish handoff, and review continuation truth.
Backend preference, routing choice, and operability metadata.
AutoDrive decision trace and navigation-state coverage.
Runtime evaluation profile and scenario-profile coverage for AutoDrive.
Replay gap resync and event durability assertions.
Recoverable runtime/provider failure sequences with deterministic follow-up proof.
Deterministic write-safe workspace mutations with machine-readable evidence.

capabilityRequirements.notes: Runtime truth starts with the flagship lane before broader rollout.
Recovery coverage stays visible on flagship and compatibility coding routes.
Write-safe gating stays anchored to the flagship route.
Replay gap resync stays live across flagship, mini, and coding lanes.
AutoDrive navigation truth stays visible on flagship and compatibility coding routes.
AutoDrive evaluation-profile truth stays visible on flagship and compatibility coding routes.

scenarioRequirements.notes: Read-only coverage keeps flagship and coding-route inspection paths healthy.
Streaming queue/resume coverage proves flagship and mini routing stay replay-safe.
Recovery coverage spans the active flagship path and the compatibility coding route.
Isolation checks must cover both the flagship default and the current mini tier.
AutoDrive launch truth is replayed on flagship and compatibility coding routes.
Safe write-path baseline remains anchored to the flagship route.
Edge routing exercises both compatibility coding behavior and the current mini route.
